steps.title: Use the x-intercept meaning
steps.content: An $x$-intercept is a point where the graph crosses the $x$-axis. At an $x$-intercept, the output is $0$.

steps.title: Convert the given intercepts
steps.content:
The given intercepts are $(-4,0)$, $(5,0)$, and $(12,0)$. These points mean the graph's output is $0$ at those three input values. Therefore
$$f(-4)=0$$
$$f(5)=0$$
$$f(12)=0$$

steps.title: Match the must-be-true statement
steps.content: The statement that must be true is $f(12)=0$. The answer is C.
